title: Tongoloa napifera
taxonomy: Tongoloa napifera is a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It is native to Australia and is also known as the swamp parsnip. The plant is classified as a perennial herb, growing in wetlands and along watercourses. Its classification is further divided into the genus Tongoloa and the family Apiaceae, which is commonly known as the carrot family. The plant's unique classification within this family is due to its distinct characteristics and growth habits.

intro: Tongoloa napifera, also known as the Yellow Wild Ginger, is a plant species native to Africa, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and edible rhizomes.
